perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information provided by the Company in accordance with this Agreement is for MARIPOSA’s use in the manufacture of the Company’s products only. MARIPOSA shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information other than as reasonably necessary to acco mplish these purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information provided by the Company in accordance with this Agreement is for MARIPOSA’s use in the manufacture of the Company’s products. MARIPOSA shall disclose any of the Confidential Information as reasonably necessary to accomplish these purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that MARIPOSA 'shall not disclose' Confidential Information, with an exception for uses 'as reasonably necessary'. By changing 'shall not disclose' to 'shall disclose,' we create a direct contradiction. The modified clause mandates disclosure which creates an ambiguity over what must be disclosed and what should remain confidential as originally suggested.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Restricted Use. Confidential Information provided by the Company in accordance with this Agreement is for MARIPOSA’s use in the manufacture of the Company’s products. MARIPOSA shall disclose any of the Confidential Information as reasonably necessary to accomplish these purposes. Notwithstanding the preceding, the Company understands and agrees as follows: (a) MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law or regulation; (b) MARIPOSA’s nonuse and nondiscl osure obligations above shall not apply to such Confidential Information as: (1) was publicly known prior to disclosure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (2) became publicly known, without fault on the part of MARIPOSA, subsequent to disclos ure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (3) was received by MARIPOSA at any time from a source, other than the Company, lawfully having possession of the right to disclose such information; or (4) was otherwise known by MARIPOSA prior to disclo sure by the Company to MARIPOSA of such information; (c) The product formulas used for the Company products may be similar to formulas used by other manufacturers, and it is unlikely that any such formulas would qualify for patent or other intellectual property protection; (d) MARIPOSA may manufacture and sell products that use formulas that are similar to (but not identical with) formulas used for the Company products. In this regard, a formula shall not be considered identical if the chemical components of the f ormula vary in any respect.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that MARIPOSA 'shall not disclose' Confidential Information, with an exception for uses 'as reasonably necessary'. The changed text modifies this to 'shall disclose' any of the Confidential Information as reasonably necessary, which directly contradicts the initial intention of maintaining confidentiality, creating a strong in-text contradiction in the agreement. The contradicted text attempts to lay out exemptions to the original nondisclosure agreement, but the changed text now mandates disclosure. This creates a clear conflict and uncertainty regarding Mariposa's disclosure obligations making it a Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as defined.

perturbation.original_text: Information that is not marked or otherwise identified a s confidential at the time of disclosure to MARIPOSA shall be treated as Confidential Information if it is identified as confidential in a written document received by MARIPOSA within thirty (30) days after initial disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Information that is not marked or otherwise identified a s confidential at the time of disclosure to MARIPOSA shall not be treated as Confidential Information even if it is identified as confidential in a written document received by MARIPOSA within thirty (30) days after initial disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The change makes unmarked information non-confidential, regardless of later written notification. This contradicts the original provision that allowed for a 30-day window to retroactively classify information as confidential, this creates an in-text contradiction within section 2.1 of what should be considered confidential
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Definition of Confidentiality Information. “Confidential Information” shall mean the formulas and related information supplied t o MARIPOSA by the Company but only to the extent that: (a) Such information is maintained as confidential by the Company, and; (b) Marked or otherwise identified as confidential when disclosed to MARIPOSA. Information that is not marked or otherwise identified a s confidential at the time of disclosure to MARIPOSA shall not be treated as Confidential Information even if it is identified as confidential in a written document received by MARIPOSA within thirty (30) days after initial disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that information not initially marked as confidential will 'not' be treated as confidential, even if later identified in writing. This directly contradicts the implicit possibility in the original text and the structure of section 2.1 of the contract that confidentiality 'can' be established retroactively with written notification within 30 days, creating a direct conflict in what qualifies as confidential information. This directly violates structural flaw - In Text Contradiction as the same clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: MARIPOSA may manufacture and sell products that use formulas that are similar to (but not identical with) formulas used for the Company products. In this regard, a formula shall not be considered identical if the chemical components of the f ormula vary in any respect.
perturbation.changed_text: MARIPOSA may manufacture and sell products that use formulas that are similar to (but not identical with) formulas used for the Company products. In this regard, a formula shall be considered identical even if the chemical components of the formula vary in any respect.
perturbation.explanation: The original states formulas are not identical if chemical components vary. The modified version states that formulas can be considered identical even if components vary. This is a direct contradiction about what constitutes an identical formula. This perturbation is in text contradiction because within the same section we are stating to conflicting definition of what consistitute an indetical formula.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Restricted Use. Confidential Information provided by the Company in accordance with this Agreement is for MARIPOSA’s use in the manufacture of the Company’s products only. MARIPOSA shall not disclose any of the Confidential Information other than as reasonably necessary to acco mplish these purposes. Notwithstanding the preceding, the Company understands and agrees as follows: (a) MARIPOSA shall have the right to disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by applicable law or regulation; (b) MARIPOSA’s nonuse and nondiscl osure obligations above shall not apply to such Confidential Information as: (1) was publicly known prior to disclosure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (2) became publicly known, without fault on the part of MARIPOSA, subsequent to disclos ure by the Company of such information to MARIPOSA; (3) was received by MARIPOSA at any time from a source, other than the Company, lawfully having possession of the right to disclose such information; or (4) was otherwise known by MARIPOSA prior to disclo sure by the Company to MARIPOSA of such information; (c) The product formulas used for the Company products may be similar to formulas used by other manufacturers, and it is unlikely that any such formulas would qualify for patent or other intellectual property protection; (d) MARIPOSA may manufacture and sell products that use formulas that are similar to (but not identical with) formulas used for the Company products. In this regard, a formula shall be considered identical even if the chemical components of the f ormula vary in any respect.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself within the same section. The original text stated that formulas are not considered identical if chemical components vary. The changed text states the opposite - that formulas can be considered identical even if the chemical components vary. This directly creates an in-text contradiction as it provides conflicting definitions of what constitutes an identical formula within the same section of the agreement. The contradicted text in 2.2(d) now has conflicting meanings depending on which version of the definition of 'identical' is used.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to any express provisions in this Agreement regarding restrictions on transfers or assignments, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors, assigns, heirs, and personal representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding any express provisions in this Agreement regarding restrictions on transfers or assignments, this Agreement shall not be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors, assigns, heirs, and personal representatives.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'Subject to' with 'Notwithstanding', the clause directly contradicts itself and throws into question whether the agreement actually binds successors/assigns. This creates confusion and in-text contradiction on the enforceability against successors.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.3 Successors and Assigns. Notwithstanding any express provisions in this Agreement regarding restrictions on transfers or assignments, this Agreement shall not be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors, assigns, heirs, and personal representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by replacing 'Subject to' with 'Notwithstanding,' directly negates the original clause's intent. The contradicted text repeats the changed text verbatim. Both clauses now state that the agreement 'shall not be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective successors, assigns, heirs, and personal representatives,' directly conflicting with the initial intent to bind successors and assigns under certain conditions as described in the original text before it was changed. Therefore, this change creates a strong in-text contradiction, rendering the agreement's binding nature on successors uncertain. The structural flaw causes in-text contradiction by placing a clause in two locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Time is of the essence with respect to the obligations to be performed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Time is not of the essence with respect to the obligations to be performed under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that 'Time is not of the essence', the clause reverses the explicit and more common legal meaning regarding deadlines and timely performance. The original text states that missing the deadlines is a material breach, and if we make time is not of the essence, it makes a contradiction within the implied conditions within the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Time of the Essence. Time is not of the essence with respect to the obligations to be performed under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "Time is of the essence", while the changed text states the opposite, "Time is not of the essence". The contradicted text also states "Time is not of the essence". While the changed text and contradicted text have the same statement, this is a Structural Flaw because the original text contradicts the changed text, causing an in-text contradiction.
